response.justification: The candidate is a polyglot generalist with deep roots in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, but lacks the core competencies this role demands. Java appears only peripherally in two older roles, Kotlin is absent entirely, and there is no evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise JVM framework experience. While the candidate has built developer tooling (test runners, testing libraries in DLang) and once contributed C components to an IDE product, these do not translate to the JVM internals and enterprise IDE subsystem work required here.
strengths: Has built developer tooling and testing frameworks from scratch (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), demonstrating genuine interest in the tooling space
Demonstrated ability to work across a wide range of paradigms and languages, suggesting adaptability
Experience writing low-latency, high-throughput systems shows engineering depth beyond typical web work
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only superficial Java usage — the two non-negotiable technical foundations of this role are effectively absent
Zero exposure to the Spring ecosystem, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework across 15+ years of employment history
Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js; the candidate's IDE-adjacent experience (NI LabWindows) was writing C components for an IDE, not building IDE internals or JVM tooling

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise JVM frameworks
